title: Point2D Addition
prompt_md:
## Goal

Overload `operator+` as a **member function** of a `Point2D` class so that `a + b` produces a new point whose coordinates are the component-wise sums.

## Input

- One line with four integers `x1 y1 x2 y2` (each between -1000 and 1000).

## Output

- A single line `(x, y)` giving the sum of the two points.

The operator must be a `const`-qualified member function (the left operand is the implicit `*this`, so it takes one explicit parameter). Do not implement it as a friend or free function.

title: Fraction Multiplication
prompt_md:
## Goal

Overload `operator*` as a **member function** of a `Fraction` class. Multiplying two fractions must produce a new fraction in **reduced form** (the constructor already reduces via a provided GCD helper, so a product built from the right numerator and denominator will be reduced automatically).

## Input

- One line with four integers `num1 den1 num2 den2` (numerators between -100 and 100, denominators between 1 and 100).

## Output

- A single line `num/den` -- the product in reduced form. A negative result keeps the sign on the numerator.

The operator must be a `const`-qualified member function (not a friend or free function).

test_cases.label: Result reduces to whole number fraction
